From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [The Village Recorder](/w/index.php?title=The_Village_Recorder&redirect=no "The Village Recorder"))

Recording studio in Los Angeles

The Village

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Exterior of the studio as of April 2021 | |

**The Village** (also known as **Village Recorders**, or **the Village Recorder**) is a [recording studio](/wiki/Recording_studio "Recording studio") located at 1616 Butler Avenue in [West Los Angeles](/wiki/West_Los_Angeles "West Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Village_(studio)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The building was built by the [Freemasons](/wiki/Freemasonry "Freemasonry") in 1922[[1]](#cite_note-soundonsound-1) and was originally a Masonic temple. It remained that way until the 1960s, when [Maharishi Mahesh Yogi](/wiki/Maharishi_Mahesh_Yogi "Maharishi Mahesh Yogi") used the building as a center for [Transcendental Meditation](/wiki/Transcendental_Meditation "Transcendental Meditation").[[1]](#cite_note-soundonsound-1)

The temple was converted into a recording studio in 1968 by composer and meatpacking heir [Geordie Hormel](/wiki/Geordie_Hormel "Geordie Hormel").[[1]](#cite_note-soundonsound-1)

The Village is home to a vintage [Neve 8048](/wiki/Neve_8048 "Neve 8048") console (originally from [Universal Recording](/wiki/Universal_Recording_Corporation "Universal Recording Corporation") in Chicago) as well as two Neve 88R consoles. The Village is renowned for its extensive inventory of vintage microphones and [outboard gear](/wiki/Outboard_gear "Outboard gear"). The studio also has [Oscar Peterson](/wiki/Oscar_Peterson "Oscar Peterson")'s [Steinway & Sons](/wiki/Steinway_%26_Sons "Steinway & Sons") Model L, which Peterson used extensively from the 1940s through the 1980s.

Many major motion picture and television soundtracks have also been recorded at the studio, including *[Ace Ventura](/wiki/Ace_Ventura "Ace Ventura")*, *[Dead Poets Society](/wiki/Dead_Poets_Society "Dead Poets Society")*, *[O Brother, Where Art Thou?](/wiki/O_Brother,_Where_Art_Thou%3F "O Brother, Where Art Thou?")*, *[The Rose](/wiki/The_Rose_(film) "The Rose (film)")*, *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")*, *[Toy Story 2](/wiki/Toy_Story_2 "Toy Story 2")*, *[Walk the Line](/wiki/Walk_the_Line "Walk the Line")*, *[The X-Files](/wiki/The_X-Files "The X-Files")*, *[Wall-E](/wiki/Wall-E "Wall-E")*, *[Revolutionary Road](/wiki/Revolutionary_Road_(film) "Revolutionary Road (film)")*, *[The Shawshank Redemption](/wiki/The_Shawshank_Redemption "The Shawshank Redemption")*, *[Avatar: The Way of Water](/wiki/Avatar:_The_Way_of_Water "Avatar: The Way of Water")* and others.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The Village becomes home to [KCRW](/wiki/KCRW "KCRW")'s *[Morning Becomes Eclectic](/wiki/Morning_Becomes_Eclectic "Morning Becomes Eclectic")* radio show, hosted by [Jason Bentley](/wiki/Jason_Bentley "Jason Bentley"), during on-air membership drives when the station's own performance room is unavailable.
